4. Review the Quality Gates

  • Check the predefined Quality Gates and schedule them for the right timebox
  • Create additional Quality Qates with Checklists

5. Additional functionalities

Use other desired functions such as Requirements Management, Test Management, Release Management, etc.

entry.title: An Introduction to Documentation Versions on the SAP Help Portal for Functional Localization
entry.content:
I’m pleased to share the latest information on accessing documentation on the SAP Help Portal for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. In this blog post, I’ll explain how you can choose the right documentation version for your technical requirements and discuss the recent updates on the SAP Help Portal for maintenance phases.

Title image for the topic of documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal

I’ll be focusing today on the country/region-specific documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition that is published through the Documentation Delivery with SAP Notes process, though there are useful tips here for everyone who browses version-specific content on the SAP Help Portal.

1. Choosing the Right Documentation Version for You

The SAP Help Portal is your number-one resource for information on SAP products. When navigating documentation, it’s important to verify that the version you’re browsing is correct for your solution. You can do this by checking the Version Selector drop-down menu, as shown in this example from the Local Versions page:

Drop-down version selection on the SAP Help Portal

Why do we recommend this version verification step? Well, you can access SAP Help Portal documentation by many different means, such as links from SAP Notes and What’s New topics, as well as from search engines. You may be directed to the highest version, which is not necessarily the same version implemented by your company. Different versions may have different scope functionality described in the documentation, so it’s best to always check this drop-down menu when accessing the SAP Help Portal.

2. New on the SAP Help Portal: Maintenance Information

You might have noticed that, when you access documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, there’s a new information banner that specifies the maintenance phase:

Maintenance message now available on the SAP Help Portal

This information banner differentiates between two maintenance phases, Mainstream Maintenance and Extended Maintenance. If you are an SAP customer or partner with a valid SAP S-User ID, you can refer to the SAP Maintenance Phases resource for further details on this topic.

For the most recent documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal, you are informed that “documentation is updated with the support package stacks”. However, there is an important exception to this rule for country/region-specific updates delivered for functional localization.

3. Always Up-to-Date: Documentation Delivery for Functional Localization

For country/region-specific development delivered for functional localization, such as legal changes, a new process provides documentation updates between support package stacks. As detailed in the recent blog post on Documentation Delivery on the SAP Help Portal with SAP Notes, you now have access to SAP Help Portal documentation that reflects the latest information, regardless of the planned support package release dates.  

For both the mainstream and extended maintenance phases of SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we’re glad to provide you with up-to-date information on how SAP meets the local legal requirement for your solution. 

To find more information on country/region-specific topics, I recommend the Localization topic page on the SAP Community. There you can find the latest news, resources, and upcoming events for SAP’s localized solutions. Remember, too, that you can always select the Create a Question option on the Localization topic page to engage with experts and resolve any doubts you may have:

Create a Question for Localization on the SAP Community

And for those of you who use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, you can find more details on the SAP Help Portal and SAP Community in our dedicated blog series:

  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Engaging with the SAP Community

That’s it for today, though please let me know in the comments section if you have any questions. Your feedback is always appreciated.

Thank you and until next time,

Barnabas

entry.title: Performing AMDP CURD OPERATIONS
entry.content:
AMDP CURD OPERATIONS 

 AMDP CURD Operations: 

AMDP (ABAP Managed Database Procedures) is a feature in SAP HANA that allows you to write database procedures in SQL Script directly within the ABAP environment. AMDP provides a seamless way to execute database-level operations from ABAP while leveraging the power of HANA's in-memory capabilities. When it comes to CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete), here's how they are typically handled with AMDP: 

Why Use AMDP for CRUD Operations? 

Performance: AMDPs run directly on the database layer, making them faster than traditional ABAP for complex operations. 

Complex Scenarios: 
AMDP can handle bulk data operations efficiently, such as processing thousands of orders in real time. 

Seamless Integration: 

AMDP allows developers to stay in the ABAP environment while leveraging HANA’s advanced database features like parallel processing. 

Business Scenarios: 

A manufacturing company wants to streamline its sales process to ensure accurate order fulfillment and improve customer satisfaction. The company uses AMDP in the SD module to handle the following tasks: 

  1. Sales Order Creation: Automatically reserve stock or trigger purchase requisitions if stock is insufficient. 
  1. Order Status Tracking: Provide real-time updates on sales order status. 
  1. Delivery Management: Update the status of deliveries once completed. 

 Steps to achieve AMDP CURD Operations: 

Create a AMDP class.

Most important part is here is, if you want to use any class with AMDP’s, you have to implement “ if_amdp_marker_hdb” interface. 

So when you are writing the method of the class you have to add some keywords with which tables you are going to use, if not you cannot do any operations on these tables. 

So we add both method declarations and table names to our methods. 

Create operation: 

 

METHOD create_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking. 

it_insert_data = SELECT 'BID0000103', 'RUSHITHA', 'KADAPA', 'BANGALORE', 100, ' ' frOM dummy ; 
inSERT iNTO zgr_t_booking seLECT * FROM :it_insert_data ; 

ENDMETHOD.

 

Output: 

Update Operation: 

 

METHOD update_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

UPDATE zgr_t_booking SET passenger_name = 'RESHMA' WHERE booking_id = 'BID0000103' ; 

ENDMETHOD.

 

Output: 

Read Operations: 

 

METHOD read_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking. 

it_booking = SELECT * FROM zgr_t_booking ; 

ENDMETHOD.

 

Output: 

Delete Operation: 

 

METHOD delete_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking. 

DELETE fROM zgr_t_booking WHERE booking_id = 'BID0000101' ; 

ENDMETHOD.

 

Output: 

AMDP CURD operation Entire Logic :

 

CLASS zcl_gr_amdp_curd DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    TYPES: tt_booking TYPE TABLE OF zgr_t_booking.

    INTERFACES if_amdp_marker_hdb.
    CLASS-METHODS : create_data,
      update_data,
      read_data EXPORTING VALUE(it_booking) TYPE tt_booking,
      delete_data.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_gr_amdp_curd IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD create_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

    it_insert_data = SELECT 'BID0000103', 'RUSHITHA', 'KADAPA', 'BANGALORE', 100,'' frOM dummy ;

    inSERT iNTO zgr_t_booking seLECT * FROM :it_insert_data ;

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD update_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

    UPDATE zgr_t_booking SET passenger_name = 'RESHMA' WHERE booking_id = 'BID0000103' ;

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD read_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

    it_booking = SELECT * FROM zgr_t_booking ;

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

    DELETE fROM zgr_t_booking WHERE booking_id = 'BID0000101' ;

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Method Chaining: A Modern Approach to Simplifying SAP ABAP Code
entry.content:
What Is Method Chaining?  

Method Chaining in ABAP allows you to call multiple methods one after the other in a single statement, without needing helper variables. This makes your code cleaner and easier to read. 

Previously, ABAP only supported chaining of attributes in class calls. But starting with ABAP 7.0 EhP2, you can also chain methods along with attributes.  

 Key Features of Method Chaining in SAP ABAP 

  1. Chained Execution: Allows multiple methods to be executed in a single statement. 
  2. Fluent Interface Design: Methods return the same object (SELF), enabling seamless chaining of operations. 
  3. Enhanced Readability: Eliminates the need for intermediate variables, making the code cleaner and more intuitive. 
  4. Concise Code: Reduces the number of lines of code, improving maintainability. 
  5. Modern ABAP Practice: Aligns with modern programming paradigms and best practices.
  6. Ease of Debugging: Individual methods can be tested independently while supporting chaining.

Advantages of Method Chaining 

Improved Readability: Code is more concise and easier to understand. 

Fewer Variables: Reduces the need for intermediate variables.

Aligned with OOP: Encourages a clean and modern ABAP development style. 

Reusable Code: Enables building reusable and modular classes.

Fluent Interface: Helps developers create a more intuitive and fluent API for their classes. 

Example for Method Chaining: 

I have taken 3 classes and one method in each class and given implementations for all three methods of a Class. 

 

 

REPORT zch_rp_method_chaining.
CLASS lcl_discount DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS apply_discount IMPORTING im_price                   TYPE i
                                     im_discount                TYPE i
                           RETURNING VALUE(re_discounted_price) TYPE i.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_tax DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS apply_tax IMPORTING im_price                 TYPE i
                                im_tax_rate              TYPE i
                      RETURNING VALUE(re_price_with_tax) TYPE i.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_final_price DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS calculate_final_price IMPORTING im_price              TYPE i
                                  RETURNING VALUE(re_final_price) TYPE i.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_discount IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD apply_discount.
    re_discounted_price = im_price - ( im_price * im_discount / 100 ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_tax IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD apply_tax.
    re_price_with_tax = im_price + ( im_price * im_tax_rate / 100 ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_final_price IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD calculate_final_price.
    re_final_price = im_price.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

Without Method Chaining if we want to call the method :

We need to create Object reference for each class and using that reference we need to call the method.   

apply_discount Method: Accepts two parameters and returns a result.  

apply_tax Method: Takes the result of  apply_discount method as input for im_price .  

calculate_final_price : Uses the result of apply_tax as input for im_price and returns the final output

 

 

START-OF-SELECTION.
data : lo_discount type REF TO lcl_discount,
      lo_tax type ref to lcl_tax,
      lo_final_price type ref to lcl_final_price.

data : lv_discounted_price type i,
      lv_price_with_tax type i,
      lv_final_price type i.

CREATE OBJECT : lo_discount,
                lo_tax,
                lo_final_price.

"without method chaining
lv_discounted_price = lo_discount->apply_discount(
                        im_price    = 500
                        im_discount = 10
                      ).
lv_price_with_tax = lo_tax->apply_tax(
                      im_price    = lv_discounted_price
                      im_tax_rate = 5
                    ).
lv_final_price = lo_final_price->calculate_final_price( im_price =  lv_price_with_tax ).

write :/'Final price without method chaining ',lv_final_price.

 

With Method Chaining 

 

"With method chaining
lv_final_price = lo_final_price->calculate_final_price(
                  EXPORTING im_price = lo_tax->apply_tax(
                  EXPORTING im_price = lo_discount->apply_discount(
                                                    im_price = 500
                                                    im_discount = 10 ) im_tax_rate = 5 ) ).

write  'Final price with method chaining ',lv_final_price.

 

 

We can notice in the above example, 3 lines of code and 3 helper variables are converted to 1 method chain and 1 return variable. 

Method Chaining Structure: 

  • Innermost Call (apply_discount): 
    Calculates the discounted price: 500 - (500 * 10 / 100) = 450. 
  • Middle Call (apply_tax): 
    Applies tax to the discounted price: 450 + (450 * 5 / 100) = 473. 
  • Outermost Call (calculate_final_price): 
    Returns the final calculated price: 473. 

 Important Points

  1. For Method chaining, the system would start processing from the Inner Most method call to outer most method. 
  2. Chained Method Call Requires Functional Methods i.e they must have a RETURNING parameter .
    The Accepting Method Should Have Importing Parameters , If there are multiple importing parameters, they should be optional so the system can decide which parameter to use when chaining or need to use the EXPORTING keyword to make parameter assignments clear.
  3. The RETURNING parameter from one method must match the type of the importing parameter in the next method.
    If the types are different but compatible, the system will automatically     convert the value. 
    1. In dynamic method calls, parameter handling is slower because the system dynamically determines the method at runtime. 
      Ex: CALL METHOD lo_obj->(method_name) 
                EXPORTING  
                   im_price = 1000 
                   im_discount = 10 
              IMPORTING  
                  re_discounted_price = lv_final_price.         
      SAP Recommendation: Use helper variables to store intermediate results instead of chaining methods dynamically. 
      Ex: DATA: lv_discounted_price TYPE i. 
      lv_discounted_price = lo_sales->apply_discount(  
                              im_price = 1000  
                              im_discount = 10 ). 

  Debugging: 

In Debugging we have a tab called Auto in variable display tool. This tab would show you the step details whenever a method chain would be executed 

When you execute the statement using F6, you would notice 3 steps entry created in the Auto tab.
We can see here, system has started processing from the Inner Most method call to outer most    method. 

 

Conclusion 
Method chaining in ABAP is a powerful technique for writing clean and modern code. It enhances readability and aligns with best practices in object-oriented programming. Whether you're building complex queries, configuring objects, or designing APIs, method chaining can simplify your ABAP code and improve its maintainability.

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partners
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing options for Brazil allows you to send DANFE (NF-e) and DACTE (CT-e) in PDF format to your business partners via email. For SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP, you use the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction to display the distribution status of the XML and PDF files for each electronic fiscal document.

Take a look at the updates in our What's New session.

Instructions to generate and submit the PDF with the XML file:

1 - Follow the manual instructions in SAP Note 3532295 to customize the attached form template (template.xdp) using the Adobe LiveCycle Designer. The file is a sample that can be used to create the template for the DANFE (NF-e). The maximum template size is set to 2 MB; remember to resize or compress images used in the file.

Editing the sample file in Adobe LiveCycle Designer.

2 - After creating a new template as an XDP file in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer, use a tool to encode the file to Base64 and save it to your computer. Since an XDP file has an XML format, you can copy its source code using a simple text editor before encoding it.

The content of an XDP file opened in a text editor.

3 - You must use an HTTP client or requests via curl to update the template in the service. In the following instructions, an HTTP client is used to create the requests (GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE) and set the endpoint to the URL https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com. Refer to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for further details.

3.1 – The following process relies on token authentication (also known as bearer authentication), which is used over HTTPS (SSL). This authentication allows you to access URLs of our service. The client must send the token in the authorization header when making requests to protected resources:

 

Authorization: Bearer <token>

 

For more details about token authentication in the SAP BTP, check the following pages:

  • OAuth JWT Bearer Authentication
  • OAuth 2.0 – Understanding Token Exchange

3.2 - In the HTTP client, create a POST request with the following details:

  • URL: https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com/pdf-service/v1/templates
  • Method: POST
  • Auth: Input the generated authentication token to communicate with our cloud services. Use your clientId and clientSecret to get the token in the SAP BTP.

3.3 – In the Body, you must send the content using the HTTP client, filling in the following details:

  • Template name in templateName field, which can be whatever you want.
  • Electronic document type in documentType field, which must be filled in with “NFE” for DANFE or “CTE” for DACTE.
  • Action type in actionType field, filled in with “AUTHORIZE”.
  • Country in country field, filled in with “BR”.
  • Electronic document layout in the version field; this is an optional field, and in the example, it is filled in with “4.00”, which is the current NF-e (DANFE) layout version.
  • Environment Type in environmentType field; this is an optional field, and it is filled in with “HOMOLOGATION” for testing purposes, or “PRODUCTION”.
  • The content of the XDP template, encoded to Base64, must be pasted into xdpTemplate field.

 

{
	"templateName": "Name of the template",
	"documentType": "NFE",
	"actionType": "AUTHORIZE",
	"country": "BR",
	"version": "4.00",
	"environmentType": "HOMOLOGATION",
	"xdpTemplate":
"Content of the template encoded to Base64 here"
}

 

Template update using an HTTP client.

Note: There is a restriction for documentType, actionType, country, version, and environmentType; you should not send two or more templates that have the same values in these fields.

4 - Open the following pages in SAP Help to understand how to update the email addresses of your business partners and confirm that jobs are scheduled in your backend system.

  • Configuring Email Addresses to Send XML and PDF Files
  • Scheduling Jobs for NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e

5 - Submit an NF-e document to your business partner in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction. When the document is authorized by SEFAZ, the Distribution Log column will be updated.

6 - Click on the icon in the Distribution Log column to check the details of the email sent in Distribution History > Log. For more details, check the Distribution Bounce Statuses page in SAP Help.

Steps in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction.

The email with the PDF and XML files will be received by your business partner.

Note: After updating the template, it may take up to 15 minutes for the new version to be used, after which your business partners will receive the new PDF version and XML file via email.

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the component LOD-LH-NFE (Outbound invoicing option for Brazil); just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago


Tags: brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, electronic document, electronic documents, eletronic document, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, eletronica, electronic nota fiscal, factura electrónica, conhecimento de transporte eletrônico, nf-e, ct-e

entry.title: Credit Management for Advanced Execution Order Item via Adhoc Billing
entry.content:
This blog will help you to understand how Adhoc Billing takes place in advanced execution order item.

In the billing solution adapted for S/4 Hana Service Transactions, known as Service Orders (SO) will be via Billing via Billing Plan After Release (Adhoc Billing). During this process user must create the bill plan.

Once the billing plan is added and service order is released, it will qualify for Credit Commitment update, Hence credit management integration process needs to be triggered, Following are the key categories where credit exposures movement takes place. Transaction code to view Credit Exposures: “UKM_COMMITMENTS”.

Type of Credit Exposures Category

Description of Credit Exposures Category

110

Service Order & Service Contract

410

Service Confirmations

500

Billing Document Value

200

Open Invoices

There are different billing relevance in service order for which credit management get triggered.

In this blog we will focusing more “Billing via Billing Plan After Release”.

Customizing for credit check:

SPRO---Service--- Transactions---- Basic Settings----- Credit Management----

Make sure the transaction type is enabled for credit check.

Make sure the service order item category is also credit check enable.

To create the service order user must assigned the service manager role to their user: SAP_BR_SERVICE_MANAGER.

Once the role is assigned to the user profile. Manage Service Order’s app can be accessible

Create the service order with advanced execution order item and add the billing relevance as :“Billing via Billing Plan After Release”.

Once the user added the bill relevance as “Billing via Billing Plan After Release”,Bill Plan needs to be added manually to the service order item. Here user can add the bill plan via billing percentage as well.

After creating the service order, user must manually release the item. On released action credit check process is getting triggered to check whether the user is having sufficient credit to create the new service order or to release the item. If the credit check is not successful, then service order will go into DCD case (Documented Credit Decisions). In this case user must go via approval process to get rid of the DCD blocker. Only authorized person can approve the DCD case for blocked service order depends upon the business.To approve the DCD, user should assign the below business role to their user profile.

SAP_BR_CREDIT_CONTROLLER

Once the role is assigned user can access the Manage Documented Credit Decisions” app. Go to the “Manage Documented Credit decisions” app.

Enter the service order and click on release to approve the service order.Once the service order is approved then item will set it in “RELEASED” status.

Further integration with the Credit Management process gets trigger and update the credit exposure commitments in the 110 (Service Order & Service Contract). 

To check the credit exposures: Run the transaction UKM_COMMITMENTS. Or go the Fiori app “Display Credit Exposure”. To access the” Display Credit Exposure” application user must have “Credit Controller” role assigned to their user. Enter the details as shown below. In this case Business Partner is “17100001” and credit segment is “1000”. Click on execute

On successful execution, Credit commitment data will be updated in the 110 category.

Create the BDR for service order 8000050264. To create the BDR, User should follow the below path:

There were two billing plans added to the service order item page. Hence two BDR’s got created.

Same can be view in transaction history of service order page.

Post the Invoice via VF01.

Post the second Invoice.

Check the credit commitment exposures in 500 category. Newly created invoices as shown in below image. The commitment value : Net Value + Taxes.

Next step is to Release the Billing Document (Invoice) to accounting. Go to VF02. Enter the Billing Document. Click on Release to Accounting

Once the billing documents are Released to Accounting , 200 category is getting updated with the credit commitment for 90010685 and 90010686

Once the credit commitment update is moved to 200 category. No exposures remain in 500 category.

In advanced execution of a service order, credit management plays a crucial role in ensuring financial discipline and customer satisfaction.

The Adhoc billing feature in this process flow is explained in detail how the credit management work in advanced execution order item in service order.

To know more about credit management: Kindly go through the below blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/credit-management-for-advanced-execution-order-item-dp92/ba-p/13973490

entry.title: Credit Management for Advanced Execution Order Item in Financial and Contract Accounting (FICA)
entry.content:
This blog will help you to understand how business process works when it comes to the financial and contract accounting scenario in advanced execution order item.

In the billing solution adapted for S/4 Hana Service Transactions, known as Service Orders (SO) will be via Dynamic Item Processor Framework (DIP) [transaction DP92], During this process a Debit Memo Request(DMR) document is generated. The DMR that is generated out of DIP could qualify for Credit Commitment update, Hence Credit management integration process needs to be triggered, Following are the key categories where credit exposures movement takes place.Transaction code to view Credit Exposures: “UKM_COMMITMENTS”. 

Type of Credit Exposures Category

Description of Credit Exposures Category

110

Service Order & Service Contract

410

Service Confirmations

500

Billing Document Value

200

Open Invoices

There are different billing relevance in service order for which credit management gets triggered.

In this blog we will be focusing  on how credit management process takes place in FI-CA enabled system. Behaviour is the same for summarized and itemized billing relevance. In this blog will be learning the end to end  process via summarized billing relevance.

Customizing for credit check:

SPRO---Service--- Transactions---- Basic Settings----- Credit Management----

Make sure the transaction type is credit check enabled.

Make sure the service order item category is also credit check enabled.

Check the DIP Profile Setting: Transaction “ODP1” .

In FICA enabled system, Steps to activate the FICA Integration:

Summarized Billing Relevance:

To create the service order user must assigned the service manager role to their user: SAP_BR_SERVICE_MANAGER.

Once the role is assigned to the user profile. Manage Service Order’s app can be accessible.

Create the service order with advanced execution order item and add the billing relevance as “Summarized Billing” and enter the DIP Profile.

After creating the service order, user must manually release the item. On released action credit check process is getting triggered to check whether the user is having sufficient credit to create the new service order or to release the item.Further integration with the Credit Management process gets trigger and update the credit exposure commitments in the 110 (Service Order & Service Contract) categories.  

Credit commitment exposure in 110 category on service order released.

Plant Maintenance Order (PMO) will be created upon successful release of the advanced execution order item. Manual release of the PMO in transaction via “IW32”.

Posting PMO Confirmations in transaction via IW48 to create the Debit Memo Request. Adding the actual work details in IW48 and saving the entry. Go to DP92 and create the debit memo request (DMR).

Debit Memo Request (DMR) is Created.

Credit commitment exposures should get updated in 410 categories on DMR creation.

Create the Debit Memo.

As user created the debit memo, hence credit commitment exposures will move from 410 to 500 category.

Check the 410 Commitment. As the 410 exposures moved to 500 category on successful creation of debit memo. No value should persist in the 410 category

While creating the debit memo (Invoice),Invoice values will automatically send to accounting. Manual release to accounting not needed but to transfer the credit commitment data to 200 category (Open Invoices) user should follow the below steps:

Credit Management is not updated each time an open item in FI-CA is posted.Instead, FI-CA collects all the data relevant for Credit Management (on business partner level) and hands this information over to Credit Management using mass activity “Transfer Credit Data” (TA FPCM1).

Once user create the invoice (VF01). Entry will get updated to the “DFKKOBLID - Partner Messages for SAP Credit Management Update” Table.

Before user run the transaction to transfer the data to 200 category, Note the earlier commitment: Commitment Value : 246.910,97

Run the transaction “FPCM1”.

Enter the Date ID : The date on which user want to schedule the job to transfer the commitment data.

Run ID                 : Enter the uniquely identifies program run id .

Business Partner : Enter the Business Partner and select the below checkboxes as shown in below image.

Go to logs and the Problem Class : Additional Information

Save the Program Run and Execute the Schedule Program Run.

User will get the Job Scheduling Option: Select one of the option to set the Job.

Once the Program run is Completed. Check the table DFKKOBLID. No entries will exist in the table as Credit data is already moved to 200 category.No entry persists in the table.

Check the Credit Commitment Data in the 200 Category. New records stored in the table DFKKOBLID table moved to 200 category on successful program run.

Check the credit commitment date in 500 category. As all the credit data has been transferred to 200 category, all the credit commitment data removed from 500 category.

Steps to follow when Invoice is cancelled.

Cancel the invoice 90014452.

Cancel Invoice (S1) entry will get updated under transaction history tab.During cancellation of the FI-CA document, FI-CA also stores a “partner message” in table DFKKOBLID.

The ID of the partner message is the ID of the SD billing document.

If TA FPCM1 is executed with the parameters marked in below image , these entries from table DFKKOBLID are considered. This information helps FI-CA to know, which commitments need to be reduced.

Once the Program Run is Completed. Check the credit commitment date in 200 category.  65 USD has been reduced from 246,910.97 USD

This is how  credit management works in advanced execution order item in service order for FICA enabled system.

entry.title: Opportunity Management in S/4HANA Customer Management and CRM
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA CM's opportunity management enhances sales team performance while improving decision-making processes. Offering an integrated structure, it works seamlessly with various SAP modules, enabling business processes to progress more quickly and effectively.

Its customizable nature allows SAP S/4HANA CM opportunity management to meet the needs of different industries, providing businesses with both a competitive edge and long-term customer satisfaction. This system forms a solid foundation for sustainable growth by delivering cost and time savings.

Opportunity documents are primarily created to acquire customers, making them typically targeted at leads. A lead is defined as a person or business that has not yet become a customer of an organization but has the potential to be interested in its products or services. Leads are managed through roles and are registered in the system with the "Lead" role. If the opportunity is won, they are recorded as an actual customer.

 

Features

SAP CRM Interface:

  • User-friendly graphical interface.
  • Create, update, and track opportunities.

 

Status and Stage Management:

  • Monitor the current status of opportunities (e.g., open, won, lost).
  • Plan actions specific to development stages.

 

Document and Note Management: 

  • Save opportunity-related documents and notes in the system.
  • Allow team members to share information.

 

Activity Management:

  • Track and manage activities such as meetings, calls, and emails.

 

Analytics and Reporting: 

  • Generate detailed sales performance reports.
  • Integration with SAP BW and SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

Automated Workflows: 

  • Trigger-based automation (e.g., proposal or order creation).
  • Notifications and task assignments.

 

Advantages

Real-Time Data: 

  • Fast and accurate information flow.

 

Forecast Accuracy: 

  • More effective analyses.

 

Increased Efficiency: 

  • Time savings and process optimization.

 

Ease of Use: 

  • Simple and flexible user interface.

 

Deep Integration: 

  • Compatibility with SAP ECC, S/4HANA, and other SAP modules.

 

Customer Satisfaction: 

  • Improved service delivery and process tracking.

 

 

 

Adaptations

 

Sales Cycle and Sales Stages

Sales Cycle

The Sales Cycle encompasses all the steps from the beginning to the end of a sales process. This includes the initial contact with a customer, identifying opportunities, preparing proposals, making the deal, and completing the sale thereafter. SAP S/4HANA CM (Customer Management) provides various tools and modules to track and manage this process. The sales cycle is typically defined as an opportunity management process and includes all customer interactions.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Settings for Opportunities > Define Sales Cycle and Sales Stages > Define Sales Cycle:

 

Sales Stage

A Sales Stage refers to smaller, specific phases within the sales cycle. These stages represent different steps in the progression of the sale, with each stage aimed at achieving a particular goal or outcome. For example, a sales opportunity may first move to the "New" stage, then progress through stages such as "Quote Given," "In Negotiation," and "Closed-Won." Each stage includes actions taken at that point and tasks to be followed up on.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Settings for Opportunities > Define Sales Cycle and Sales Stages > Define Sales Stages:

 

Assign Sales Stages to Sales Cycles

Assigning sales stages to sales cycles determines the stages a sales opportunity progresses through as it moves through a specific sales cycle, and defines the probability of each stage. In SAP CRM, the processes related to how sales stages are assigned to sales cycles and how they are structured can be summarized as follows:

  1. Defining Sales Stages:
    In this phase, you define which sales stages will be present in each sales cycle. For example, a sales cycle may include sales stages such as "Opportunity Identification", "Proposal Preparation" and "Initial Contact".                                                                  
  2. Setting the Time Sequence:
    In this step, you specify the sequence of sales stages within the sales cycle. This order determines the progression of the opportunity through various stages until it reaches the final outcome. For example, the "Proposal Preparation" stage comes after the "Opportunity Identification" stage.                                                                                   
  3. Setting the Probability:
    The success probability is defined for each sales stage. For example, the likelihood of success during the information exchange stage of an opportunity could be 30%. This probability represents the chance of closing the opportunity at that particular stage.        
  4. Default Success Probability Settings:
    SAP transfers the success probabilities you have defined as default values to opportunities. For example, when an opportunity progresses to a specific stage, the success probability of that stage is automatically added to the opportunity.

 

NOTE - Value 0 is not transferred as the default: 

If you don't want the success probability of a sales stage to be set to 0, you can configure SAP to not transfer this value by default. To do this, you should make the following configuration in the SMOFPARSFA table for the CRMOPPORT parameter:

- Parameter Name: SALES_STAGE

- Parameter Value: NO_DEFAULT_PROB_ZERO

- Value: X

This setting ensures that a success probability of 0 is not automatically applied to new opportunities.  

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Settings for Opportunities > Define Sales Cycle and Sales Stages > Assign Sales Stages to Sales Cycles:

 

Assignment of Sales Cycle to Transaction Type

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Define Transaction Types, after following these steps, the relevant sales cycle is defined within the opportunity document type on the page that appears. The sales stages associated with the sales cycle are displayed within the document, allowing you to track the progression of the opportunity through the defined stages.

 

Sales Stage History

The changes in the sales stage within the document can be viewed from the "Sales Stage History" tab on the web UI screen.

 

Define Opportunity Group and Source

Define Opportunity Group

The opportunity group is used as a classification feature for opportunities and can also be used in analyses. This allows different business areas or company initiatives to be viewed at a global level. Depending on the selected transaction type, the relevant opportunity group is automatically assigned to the document.

For example, if your company operates on a global scale and the business processes in the U.S. differ from those in Europe, all opportunities belonging to the "Europe" group can be reviewed in a single assessment.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Define Opportunity Group and Origin > Define Opportunity Group:

 

Assignment of Opportunity Group to Transaction Type

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Define Transaction Types, after following these steps, the relevant opportunity group is defined within the opportunity document type on the page that appears. The opportunity group is set as the default for the created document.

 

Define Source for Opportunities

Defining the source for opportunities is used to record where the opportunities originate from on the document. For example, a source can be defined as a trade fair or a phone call. This way, the revenue generated from trade fairs or sales agreements can be analyzed.

Opportunities can be grouped by sources such as trade fairs, phone calls, or website interactions, and the impact of each source on the opportunities can be analyzed.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Define Opportunity Group and Origin > Define Source for Opportunities:

 

Status Reason for Opportunity

The reason for the opportunity status and status schema are important for the evaluation and analysis of opportunities. A reason explaining the result (won, lost) of an opportunity must be documented and assessed. The reason for the status changes depending on the current state of the opportunity.

Example:

  • Status: Won
  • Reason: Due to the best product

In this process, a flexible set of reasons can be defined according to the opportunity statuses using a catalog. This enables a more detailed analysis of why opportunities were won or lost.

 

Status Reason for Opportunity

The reason for the opportunity status is actually similar to the category creation process. First, a catalog of type "H Status Reason (Opportunities)" is created.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Catalogs:

  

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Code Groups and Codes for Catalogs, After following these steps, the created catalog is selected on the page that appears, and a code group is created.

 

The reasons that should appear in the document are added to the code group.

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Code Group Profiles, After following these steps, a code group profile is created by specifying the catalog, code group, and code on the page that appears.

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Code Group Profiles, After following these steps, details such as the code group profile and code groups are specified on the page that appears, and a status code profile is created.

 

Assignment of Status Reason to Transaction Type

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Assign Status Profile and Subject Profile to Business Transaction Type, After following these steps, status reasons are assigned to the document on the page that appears. This screen includes functionalities such as:

  • Listing reasons based on statuses,
  • Defining whether reason selection is mandatory or optional.

 

Creating a Sales Order from an Opportunity Document

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Copy Control for Opportunities and Sales Transactions > Copying of Transaction Types:

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Copy Control for Opportunities and Sales Transactions > Copying of Item Categories:

 

Creating Interlinked Opportunity Documents

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Define Copying Control for Opportunity Item to Opportunity:

 

Surveys Linked to Opportunity Documents

The "CRM_SURVEY_SUITE" transaction code or the web UI screen is used to access the survey editor application.

 

A survey tailored to the request and needs is prepared and activated.

 

Defining Conditions for Surveys

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Sales Methodology > Questionnaire for Opportunities, After following these steps, criteria are defined on the page that determines under what conditions the surveys will be used. These criteria are as follows:

  • Status
  • Priority
  • Source
  • Opportunity Group
  • Sales Stage
  • Organization
  • Distribution Channel
  • Department
  • Expected Sales Revenue
  • Expected Total Value
  • Currency 

 

Assigning Surveys to Opportunity Documents

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Sales Methodology > Define Determination for Questionnaires, After following these steps, the survey type is linked to the document type on the page that appears. Additionally, criteria groups, mandatory status, and validity information can also be defined.

 

Competitors

In this field, you can define which of the following you want to display on the opportunity overview page:

  • If you leave the selection blank, you can display competitors without products at header and item level.
  • If you choose Header Mode, you can display competitors with products at header level.

    In this case, the competitors and competitor products refer to the entire opportunity, not to individual items.

  • If you choose Item Mode, you can display competitors with products at item level.

    In this case, the competitors and competitor products refer to only one item, that is, you can define the corresponding competitors and competitor products separately for each item in your opportunity.

If "Header Mode" is selected:

If "Item Mode" is selected:

If the competitor mode is not selected:

 

Creating a Competitor Product

Cross-Application Components > Master Data Synchronization > Customer/Vendor Integration > Settings for Customer Integration > Field Assignment for Customer Integration > Assign Keys > Define Number Assignment for Direction BP to Customer(Table: TBD001), On the next page, the account group is matched with the business partner group. The account group should be '0006' for competitors.

 

A partner is created in the competitor group. The customer number is entered according to the appropriate number range defined in the FLCU01 role.

 

A material of type VVGR is created using the transaction code “MM01”.

 

The customer number is entered for the material using the transaction code "MM02".

 

Search Competitor Product:

 

Competitor Page:

 

Tables Related to Opportunities

  • CRMC_OPPIMPOR: Opportunity Priority / Prospect Priority
  • CRMC_OPPIMPOR_T: Opportunity Priority / Prospect Priority: Text
  • CRMC_OPPORT_H: Transaction Type - Opportunity Control Parameters
  • CRMC_OPPT_OD: Customization Table for Transaction Types (Opportunity)
  • CRMC_OPPT_TYPE: Opportunity Type
  • CRMC_OPPT_TYPE_T: Opportunity Types: Texts
  • CRMC_SVY_OPPT: Define Criteria for Surveys
  • CRMC_SVY_OPPT_T: Define Criteria for Surveys: Text
  • CRMD_DH_DATA_OPP: Opportunity Data Buffer
  • CRMD_OPP_LINK: Opportunity Linkages (Main Records)
  • CRMD_OPPORT_H: Opportunity
  • CRMD_OPPORT_I: Opportunity Item Table
  • CRMD_PPM_OPPBUF: Real-Time Buffer Fields for All Opportunities
  • CRMD_PPM_OPPBUFI: Real-Time Buffer Fields for All Opportunities (Item Level)
  • CRMS4_LCOPPTSTAT: Opportunity Life Cycle Status
  • CRMS4_OPPTASRCH: Opportunity / Prospect Search
  • CRMS4_OPPTHSRCH: Opportunity / Prospect Header Data
  • CRMS4_OPPTIMPOR: Opportunity Priority / Prospect Priority
  • CRMS4_OPPTISRCH: Opportunity / Prospect Item
  • CRMS4_OPPTSEARCH: Opportunity / Prospect Search
  • CRMS4D_OPPT_H: Opportunity and Prospect Header Details
  • CRMS4D_OPPT_I: Opportunity and Prospect Item Details
  • CRMS4V_SH_OPPT_H: Documents by Object Number
  • CRMV_OPPIMPOR: Opportunity / Prospect Priority
  • CRMV_OPPORT_H: Opportunity Customization at Header Level
  • CRMV_OPPORT_ITEM: Opportunity Customization at Item Level
  • CRMV_OPPT_OD: Transaction Types for Opportunity OData Service
  • CRMV_OPPT_TYPE: Opportunity Group
  • CRMV_SVY_DET_OPPT: Define Survey Criteria (Opportunity)
  • CRMV_SVY_OPPT: Define Survey Criteria for Opportunities

     

 

Restrictions with S/4HANA CM

The following functionalities are not supported in Opportunity Management:

  1. Purchasing Organization: It is not possible to define a purchasing organization at the account level and use it as default in the opportunity. Additionally, the graphical representation of the purchasing organization is no longer supported.
  2. Pipeline Management (PPM).
  3. Limitations on Competitor Product Relationships.
  4. Configurable Items.

entry.title: Basics of sustainability solution SAP Product and REACH Compliance
entry.content:
Introduction

The present blog

This blog will give you a first overview to the functionality of SAP Product and REACH Compliance. This solution is available on SAP ECC as AddOn or technically embedded in SAP S/4HANA. Here you get a first flavor of the scope of the solution.

The author

I have been working since years in the area of sustainability supporting customers in implementing product compliance solutions in several industries.

Overview to previously written blogs:

2022: Reference customers and/or customers success stories for SAP Product and REACH Compliance 

2021: How to uninstall SAP Product and REACH Compliance 2.0 (to get ready for SAP S/4HANA) 

2020: Are you affected by SCIP database of ECHA? Think about SAP Product Compliance for Discrete Ind... 

 

SAP Product and REACH Compliance

Customers

In general, all companies that produce discrete items (articles) might benefit from this solution. Automotive customers can interface the <<International Material Data System>> (IMDS) for downloading material data sheets (MDS) from their suppliers and uploading MDS to their customers.

My customers, where I have implemented SAP Product and REACH Compliance, are active in the following areas: 

  • Automotive (both OEM and Tier-1)
  • Manufacturing (flood control, pump, seal, valve, energy)
  • Hot stamping and coating technology (scope is both process and discrete)
  • Aerospace and Defense
  • Medical Engineering
  • Life Science 

Scope overview to SAP Product and REACH Compliance

The figure below introduces the scope of SAP Product and REACH Compliance. 

Regulatory List Management

Regulatory lists are mainly negative substance lists that might contain listed substances or listed substance groups. A listed substance group is for example "lead" that is being used for <<Restriction of Hazardous Substances>> (ROHS) and that contains more than 700 hundred single listed substances. Negative substance list means that the substances are either prohibited or declarable (or even both). Furthermore, the regulatory list defines for each listed substance the threshold and the algorithm for calculation.

Compliance Checks (in Compliance Requirements)

Most of the Compliance Requirements such as ROHS, IMDS, REACH-SVHC*, etc. are linked with exact one regulatory list and consume the data of them to check whether a basic material, component, product, contains these substances above threshold. This check is embedded in the compliance checks where several check criteria are in that ensure that the <<Compliance Data Object>> (CDO) is compliant or not. These compliance requirements might be linked with one default normalization that is applied for all calculations (even in automatic processing)

* REACH-SVHC: Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation, and restrictions of Chemicals - Substances of Very High Concern

Basic Material Management

For own produced components, often basic materials come into place. They can be added to own components and by this the chemical composition is known as the basic materials contain pure substances**. Using external systems like IMDS basic materials can be downloaded and used in SAP Product and REACH Compliance.

** Pure substances and listed substances are different objects in SAP Product Compliance. For seamless functionality these objects must be linked when existing on both sides.

Compliance Data

SAP Product and REACH Compliance uses its own objects, the compliance data objects. These CDOs are the counterparts of the products, components, supplier-material information, etc. in logistics. Using the Bill-of-Material-Transfer (BOM-Transfer) the system transfers the relevant objects for compliance to product compliance. Where required, high sophisticated logic to retrieve the best variant of a BOM (the best plant using the best usage and alternative) can be applied. These compliance data objects are used for compliance calculation and might have several versions (to still have access to historical data) 

Compliance Declarations from suppliers

Several formats are supported out of the box as "manual data maintenance", IPC1752A (this is a standard from IPC <<Association Connecting Electronics Industries>>), and MDS from IMDS.

Compliance Declarations for customer

Out of the box the system can create MDS to IMDS, IPC1752A format C, and Adobe Interactive Forms documents such as <<Declaration of Substances>> (DoS) and <<Certificate of Compliance>> (CoC).

Compliance Reporting

Here the system offers <<Core Data Services>> (CDS) views to retrieve data using compliance analysis from the system. Besides that, Excel templates are available to retrieve data from selected products or components and transfer them to Excel.

SAP Product and REACH Compliance - scope overview

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Deeper insight to BOM Transfer

As we have already touched above, the BOM Transfer copies the relevant objects from logistics to product compliance. In case the objects do not yet exist, they are created, otherwise the system updates them.

Objects

  • Materials (system will identify them and categorize them as "package", "bulk", or "product or product part"
  • BOM Structure (system uses specification database to store this information)
  • Supplier-material objects (these supplier parts are identified for most cases via source list or purchasing info record)

BOM Transfer triggers

As the figure below shows, there are certain triggers possible to initiate this transfer.

Manual transfer

Manually started by the user. That is what SAP has foreseen for trading goods. It might help during cutover, too. But for most use cases the customers want to have a higher automatization.

Bill of Material gets created or changed

That is also the SAP standard approach, however; the used workflows are not appreciated by customers and often a BOM gets changes to a relevant status before the material gets a relevant status. So, most customers rely on our own worklist that covers several use cases.

Supplier material links have changed

This is an appreciated SAP standard approach. Once the source list or purchasing info record gets created or deleted, the single material transfer is initiated by the system.

Material has changed

Not part of SAP standard functionality. But while applying the functionality of worklist from above we can handle this.

BOM Transfer - triggers

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Applying filters for BOM Transfer

To emphasize the powerfulness of BOM Transfer we shed some light to the filter criteria. Using two existing structures, for the materials, the BOM header, and the BOM positions filters might be configurated. By doing this we only get relevant data into product compliance. Further configuration might be used for BOM alternative handling. Last but not least, there are user exits available that allow us to realize special customer requirements, e.g., to not overwrite weight data in case a manual flag is set (weight data is always a challenge...)

Degrees of freedom using BOM Transfer

There exist two poles.

Pole 1 is to transfer each and every alternative into product compliance. This ensures maximal compliance; however, leads to complexity. Some use cases do not permit this approach. 

Pole 2 is to transfer only one structure to product compliance. This might be the latest changed BOM or the BOM with the smallest alternative.

Often a realistic scenario (using different plant nodes) gets implemented as the figure below shows.

BOM Transfer - degrees of freedom

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Product Compliance structure

The product compliance structure is being displayed in the system and used by the system for the compliance calculation. 

Objects in Product Compliance structure

The figure below provides an overview of (some of) the objects being used.

Product Compliance Structure - objects

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Normalizations

Normalizations provide a special view to the product compliance structure. So you might see all leaf elements, only basic materials, elements with issues only, etc. then normalizations come into the game. 

Beyond that there exists plenty of uses cases where normalizations get applied to manipulate the product compliance structure. The figure below shows one use case: the system creates for a procured component supplier parts. For these three suppliers you have instructed them to use exact basic material for steel XYZ. The suppliers won't give any compliance information to you. You plug-in the basic material to the component. Now the system considers both the basic material and the supplier parts and states "missing data". With the "prefer basic material" normalization, the supplier parts get suppressed in case there exists a basic material.

Product Compliance Structure - normalizations

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Summary

SAP Product and REACH Compliance is a powerful tool. For my customers I have created process documentations with 200+ pages, for sure this cannot be added into a blog. However, the basic concept on a high level is given.

In case of questions or required help just contact the author.

entry.title: How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Yesterday, while presenting SAP’s financial results for FY2024, our CEO Christian Klein explained that we exceeded our cloud goals, accelerating cloud revenue and current cloud backlog growth against a much larger base. He also mentioned that half of our cloud order entry are now including AI. Clearly, our aim is to provide our customers with a suite of cloud-based applications that provides the most valuable data in business and powers unique AI capabilities. In this blog post, I would like to present how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite – both running on SAP Business Technology Platform – are at the heart of SAP’s strategy and support our customers in their journey from legacy ERPs to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.


We know that for many SAP customers, the transition to SAP S/4HANA can be challenging, because someone in the organization has added a lot of custom code to your ERP 😉. That’s why SAP has created RISE with SAP, a packaged offering that helps you transform on-premise SAP ERP systems, modernize business processes, and become more agile in the cloud. And in RISE with SAP, you will find anything you need to fulfill your needs.

Let me be a bit more specific: every company in the world uses software to run their operations. We all know the most efficient and effective way is to use standard software like our SAP S/4HANA ERP. At the same time, we are fully aware that every organization in the world is unique and gains competitive advantage through their very specific processes. This might be the way they run processes for their customers because they offer unique experiences, or the way they engage with their suppliers because they may be faster in the supply chain processes, or it might be their HR processes which allows them to retain the best talent in the market… whatever it is, that makes your company unique, it should be reflected in your software because no one likes manual processes in 2025. In the context of migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, following the Clean Core strategy is a powerful opportunity: by eliminating custom code, technical debt, and standardizing the processes and data that underpin ERP operations, your business will benefit from more best practices while being able to customize what it needs to differentiate itself. And when it comes to standardization to achieve clean core with proper extensions and integrations, SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP enter the game!

SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite add great value to your Cloud ERP

SAP Build allows you to extend and personalize SAP S/4HANA to meet your needs. It accelerates development and automation of extensions with low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. It enables stable, secure, and scalable ERP extensions that adhere to a prescribed extensibility model, with compliance to code quality standards and best practices. Tailored for every SAP customer, any size, any landscape, any industry, SAP Build is the best of suite tools for developers and key users collaborating in AI-powered ERP extension development.

Let me say it simply: SAP Build adds great value to your Cloud ERP. You will get more out of your cloud ERP investment with SAP Build. Just because it lets you easily customize, extend, integrate and automate business processes in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution to meet specific requirements while keeping the core clean. Watch our webinar “Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build: your Free Toolkit”: in an hour, you will discover how you can leverage SAP Build’s low-code, pro-code, and generative AI development tools to drive innovation, automate processes, and supercharge your S/4HANA Cloud experience.

SAP Integration Suite is an iPaaS that helps you quickly integrate on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data. For sure, it also adds value to your ERP: it connects business processes with proven pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors, and best practices. It provides upgrade-stable interfaces, proper monitoring and error resolution capabilities, and actively used and well-documented connectors.

For What Use Cases?

Let me give you very concrete examples where SAP Build is very helpful to address specific challenges in your Lead to Cash processes:

  • Digitize in-store cash sales processing
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from Excel files
  • Automate mass maintenance of billing blocks
  • Automate the approvals of return requests
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from unstructured data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Automate managing credit blocks on sales orders

and more…

I can also share many use cases that could improve your Source to Pay processes:

  • Centralize and streamline supplier communications with dedicated business sites
  • Mass maintenance of purchase contracts
  • Automate the creation of purchase requisitions from structured data in SAP S/4HANA
  • Automate the processing of paper-based invoices
  • Automatically populate missing material codes in purchase requisitions
  • Track sustainability data across your supply chain

and more…

You will find complementary use cases on our SAP Build web page.

And there is more: on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you can browse 500+ pre-built content packages for SAP Build and 600+ pre-built content packages for SAP Integration Suite. If you have developed custom code in your on-premises ERP in the past to address such use cases or specific needs, you can explore this vast catalog to see if some of these packages might be a good fit for your cloud ERP migration process. Indeed it is very important to know that SAP Build comes with pre-built content for ECC: you can use these SAP Build packages on ECC already to standardize some extensions, and then all the extensions developed on ECC with SAP Build will taken to S/4HANA during the migration.

Insights from Customers

What I presented above is not only theoretical: customers who have been through this SAP S/4HANA journey share valuable insights that can really help you. I have selected two few interesting stories, in different industries.

Sony Pictures

Let me start with Sony Pictures Networks: relying on the legacy system, SAP ECC, their highly customized and siloed infrastructure struggled to align with the company's integrated business processes. And decentralized data presented SAP challenges in consolidating business reports until the end.

Sony decided to remaster their operations for the future through next-generation ERP, with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and Enterprise Automation, combining SAP Signavio, SAP Build And SAP Integration Suite. Indeed, SAP Signavio radically accelerates business process transformation, and I engage you to read this blog post explaining how customers on SAP ECC systems can prepare for an SAP S/4HANA transformation with SAP Signavio.

“By migrating to SAP S/4HANA and adopting SAP Business Technology Platform, we can keep the core clean. This strategy allows for faster development of any new technologies or any new process rollouts and enables us to take advantage of generative AI as part of our way forward” explains Mahesh Patil, SVP and Head of IT – Infrastructure, Integration, and Data Lake, Sony Pictures Networks India. And the results are excellent:

  • 100% automated process mining, reducing effort from weeks to days
  • 50% of interfaces managed through SAP Integration Suite
  • ~85% faster mobile app creation, reducing effort from four weeks to three days

Hitachi

Would you like another good example? Let’s take Hitachi High-Tech Corporation: they replaced their highly customized multinational business landscape by investing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud in a two-tier public and private cloud model. With a fit-to-standard ethos, the company can now focus on supporting business innovation across its subsidiaries using a side-by-side approach on SAP Business Technology Platform.

“Our new policy is to fit our business processes to standard as much as possible, reducing our need for customizations” says Yuri Yasuda, Manager Corporate DX Department, Digital Transformation Business Group, Hitachi High-Tech Corporation, who shares impressive KPIs:

  • 94% reduction in customization footprint
  • 18x faster handling of solution updates
  • 100% of on-site maintenance requirements eliminated

You can also find inspiration in the S/4HANA projects that Mahindra Group, PX Energy, LEOCH, Farys, Delta Light or Prestige have successfully implemented.

Start your S/4HANA journey with the support of SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite!

I hope that you’re now convinced that SAP BTP in general and SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite in particular are very powerful to ease your migration to SAP S/4HANA. To start this journey, watch our short video and request a customized demo: together, let’s make your business unstoppable!

entry.title: Launching the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard for Your SAP Transformation Journey
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, transformation is essential. As customers embark on their journey, they require tools that provide clarity on their current position, the status of key projects, and insights that guide their next steps and strategy. This is where the RISE with SAP dashboard proves invaluable, helping organizations keep their ERP systems agile, innovative, and optimized for continuous improvement, unlocking SAP’s full potential.

What is the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard?

The RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard is designed to support businesses in their move towards a clean core approach. This approach focuses on building resilient business processes and extensions supported by seamless integration, efficient operations, and data quality. It emphasizes the adoption of standard processes and the creation of extensions for differentiation, resulting in streamlined operations and increased efficiency.

Clean core is essential to help ensure customers’ business-critical systems remain agile, cost-effective, and ready to adopt innovations that drive business forward. The RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard is designed to support customers on their transformation journey to a clean core landscape.

Key Features and Benefits

The dashboard delivers a comprehensive set of capabilities to monitor and track the status of your systems, offering valuable insights and recommendations and ensuring alignment with SAP's best practices.

Designed for lead architects, the first release of the dashboard focused on clean core dimensions and provides the following features:

  • Access a detailed overview of system adherence and optimization opportunities for production or non-production S/4HANA, directly aligned with SAP's clean core approach
  • Analyze detailed insights and data on the clean core status of your systems, hosted on the SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management). SAP Cloud ALM is a key component of our integrated toolchain, offering a robust cloud environment optimized for daily updates and frequent functionality deployments.
  • Manage system extensibility with the dashboard, to identify deviations from SAP's recommendations and prepare for future upgrades. It supports maintaining a streamlined system with minimal customizations. This ensures that your systems are ready for future upgrades and that any necessary changes are well planned and executed.

                        System View to review your current extensions

 

We are committed to continuously enhancing its features and add new persona-based views, with regular updates that introduce new capabilities for clean core dimensions and transformation accelerators.

Next Steps: How to Get Started

The RISE with SAP is available to all customers with a RISE contract. Starting today customers can now access the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard.

Want to learn more about how you can leverage this powerful tool for your SAP S/4HANA systems? Visit the SAP Help Portal to access detailed resources on the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard. There, you’ll find step-by-step guides, FAQs, and best practices to help you get the most out of this innovative tool.

Let’s transform your SAP S/4HANA environment for success!
